title: festuca cinerea var. scabrifolia
intro: Festuca cinerea var. scabrifolia, also known as a type of mountain fescue, is a perennial grass species that is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and its antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Festuca cinerea var. scabrifolia include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and aid in wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
